plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs to reduce risk.
Significantly trim concentrated positions like SMH and CRWD.
Reduce SPY to meet concentration limits and increase cash.
Add new, high-scoring long setups like ZS and XOM.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and lower drawdowns.
Expect a lower target return due to reduced risk and leverage.
conservative.read: This conservative approach focuses on capital preservation. Target return is around 1.5% per month, with significantly reduced drawdown potential. Expect lower growth due to de-risking and holding more cash.

plan: Reduce leveraged ETFs like SOXL and exit SPAL.
Trim semiconductor exposure in SMH.
Reduce SPY to meet concentration limits.
Increase stake in profitable CRWD and add quality names ASML and NVDA.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
Target return is around 2.5% per month with moderate drawdowns.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting about 2.5% per month. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding to strong tech names. Expect moderate drawdowns in line with a diversified growth strategy.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETF SOXL and consider SPAL.
Trim SMH and SPY to reallocate capital.
Add to profitable CRWD and top scanner setups ZS and GUSH.
This plan concentrates risk in high-conviction names and leverage.
Target return is aggressive at ~4% per month.
Expect significantly higher potential drawdowns and leverage decay.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for high growth (~4% per month) by increasing leverage and concentrating in top-scoring setups. It accepts much higher potential drawdowns and the risks of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Exit all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Sell volatile sector exposure like SMH.
Increase allocation to income-focused QQQI.
Reduce SPY to create a cash buffer and add defensive XOM.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly income and low drawdowns.
Target return is ~1% per month from distributions, with minimal capital risk.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes generating steady monthly distributions with low drawdowns, targeting around 1% per month. It shifts capital to income ETFs and defensive assets, reducing growth potential but also risk. Distributions are not guaranteed.
